docs.title: Applied DS, ML & AI Notebook
docs.text: Welcome — this is my running notebook on applied data science, machine learning, and AI: daily learning logs, paper notes, and project reasoning, organized around a four-quadrant map of the field.

docs.title: About this notebook
docs.text: I'm Thrisha Rajkumar, a Data Scientist / ML Engineer working across clinical AI, time-series modelling, and applied deep learning. This site is where I track what I'm reading, building, and learning — updated continuously, not a polished one-off portfolio piece.

docs.title: How it's organized
docs.text:
• Foundations — stats, classical ML, feature engineering, explainability
  • Deep Learning — CNNs, transformers, GNNs, architectures
  • Generative & Agentic AI — LLMs, diffusion, RAG, agents
  • MLOps & Applied Systems — deployment, monitoring, safety, infra

Each section links out to papers I've read, notes I've taken, and how they connect to my own project work.

docs.title: Elsewhere
docs.text:
• GitHub
  • LinkedIn

Last structural update: July 2026. Content added continuously.

docs.title: Source
docs.text: (newsletter issue / paper / notebook worked on today)

docs.text: (2-3 sentences, your own words)

docs.title: How I'd use this
docs.text: (one line — ties it to your clinical/NLP/time-series work)

docs.title: One-line take
docs.text: (this becomes Friday's LinkedIn post material)

docs.title: Deep Learning
docs.text: CNNs, RNNs, transformers, GNNs, and the architectural ideas underneath modern models (attention, state-space layers, hybrid architectures).

docs.title: Core areas
docs.text:
• Transformer architecture & self-attention
  • Sequence models for time-series (LSTM/GRU → transformer)
  • Transfer learning & fine-tuning
  • Physics-informed neural networks

docs.title: Papers logged here
docs.text:
LIST
FROM "03-Papers"
WHERE contains(tags, "deep-learning")
SORT file.ctime DESC

docs.title: My related work
docs.text:
• INSPIRE perioperative mortality transformer pipeline (Bristol)
  • Multi-Head Attention ANNs for EMI prediction (IPEC-ECCE Asia 2026)

docs.title: Foundations
docs.text: Stats, classical ML, feature engineering, boosting, explainability. This is the backbone under everything else — and where your Unilever + SHAP/LIME work already lives.

docs.title: Core areas
docs.text:
• Regression, trees, ensembles (XGBoost, LightGBM, CatBoost)
  • Feature engineering & preprocessing
  • Explainability (SHAP, LIME)
  • Classical time-series (ARIMA, Prophet)

docs.title: Papers logged here

LIST
FROM "03-Papers"
WHERE contains(tags, "foundations")
SORT file.ctime DESC

docs.title: My related work
docs.text:
• Unilever production optimisation (250+ SKUs)
  • SHAP explainability on SiC MOSFET surrogate models

docs.title: Generative & Agentic AI
docs.text: LLMs, diffusion, RAG, and agentic systems that plan and execute multi-step tasks rather than just returning a prediction. Fastest-moving quadrant — worth a light weekly touch even outside your core lane, since clinical AI is starting to get "can this be agentic" questions.

docs.title: Core areas
docs.text:
• LLM architecture & training methods
  • RAG and retrieval systems
  • Agent harnesses & tool use
  • Clinical NLP (Clinical-BERT, Clinical-T5)

docs.title: Papers logged here

LIST
FROM "03-Papers"
WHERE contains(tags, "genai")
SORT file.ctime DESC

docs.title: My related work
docs.text: • Clinical-BERT / Clinical-T5 experiments for patient feedback NLP (NIHR project)

docs.title: MLOps & Applied Systems
docs.text: Deployment, monitoring, drift detection, safety gates — treating ML like production infrastructure. This is a genuine strength area for you already.

docs.title: Core areas
docs.text:
• Model monitoring & drift detection
  • CI/CD for ML (MLflow, GitHub Actions)
  • On-device / edge inference
  • Regulatory & safety design (AI-as-medical-device)
